summary: First-iteration full-stack testing of Bridge meeting platform. Backend pytest suite (10 tests) — 10/10 passing including LiveKit JWT decode with roomJoin=true, OpenAI Realtime ephemeral token (starts with ek_), real OpenAI translation en->es (returned 'Hola, ¿cómo estás?'), TTS audio/mpeg streaming, chat persistence, room CRUD, and 10 supported languages. Frontend Playwright E2E — Landing, Lobby, Meeting flows all passing: 10 language buttons, LiveKit connection succeeded (participant count = 1, WebRTC to wss://bridge-iwkrmt98.livekit.cloud), all 9 meeting control testids present, hand raise toggles active state, language picker updates to French, chat message sent through UI and confirmed persisted in backend Mongo.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: hand-raise state and kick/muteAll/end are broadcast over LiveKit DataChannel only, not persisted — a late-joiner won't see current raised hands (acceptable for MVP but worth noting).

Meeting.jsx grabs a second getUserMedia audio stream for the translator alongside LiveKit's own mic capture — this double-captures the microphone. Works on Chrome but may cause echo/permission conflicts on some browsers/OSes; consider using LiveKit's track directly.
CORS is allow_origins=['*'] with allow_credentials=True — browsers will reject credentialed requests with wildcard; not currently used but should be tightened.
kick handler only broadcasts a 'kick' message; a malicious client can ignore it. For real enforcement use LiveKit RoomServiceClient.removeParticipant on backend.
test_credentials: None required — guest access by name + code. /app/memory/test_credentials.md does not exist (flagged, but not needed for this MVP).
seed_data_creation: Created test room 'tgc-paq-jml' with TEST_Host as well as pytest-created rooms; TTS cache entries created. Not cleaned up but harmless.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest at /app/backend/tests/test_backend.py hits real OpenAI (translate + TTS) — costs a few cents per full run. Playwright uses Chrome fake camera (green frame with rotating numbers) which is expected. Do not attempt end-to-end voice translation via Playwright — mic capture doesn't work in headless.
